items.title: China to buy 200 Boeing jets, Trump says, fewer than expected
items.summary: WASHINGTON/SEATTLE: China has agreed to order 200 Boeing jets, US President Donald Trump told the Fox News Channel on Thursday, marking the country's first purchase of US-made commercial jets in nearly a decade. Boeing did not immediately respond to a request for comment. During Trump's November 2017 trip, Beijing agreed to buy 300 Boeing aeroplanes.

items.title: Technician murder: Police seek CCTV footage in Prima Tebrau case
items.summary: JOHOR BARU: Police are seeking closed-circuit television (CCTV) footage from residents to assist investigations into the murder of a 33-year-old technician found dead in a pool of blood at an apartment unit in Prima Tebrau here on Wednesday night. Sources said most CCTV cameras at the apartment were under maintenance at the time, leaving only one at the main exit in operation. "The victim was found lying in a blood-soaked condition at 10.50pm.

items.title: Oil rises as fears of ship attacks and seizures persist
items.summary: BEIJING: Oil prices rose Friday as market concerns persisted over ship attacks and seizures despite Iran saying about 30 vessels had passed through the Strait of Hormuz, while the US and Chinese presidents were set for a second day of talks in Beijing. Brent crude oil futures rose 60 cents, or 0.57 per cent, to US$106.32 a barrel by 0100 GMT, while US West Texas Intermediate futures were up 54 cents, or 0.53 per cent, to US$101.71. "Oil prices swung several times yesterday but still closed near the day's high," he said.

items.title: Ringgit snaps seven-day rally ahead of Q1 GDP data
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: The ringgit snapped a seven-trading-day rally to ease against the US dollar as traders turned cautious ahead of Malaysia's first-quarter (1Q) 2026 gross domestic product (GDP) announcement later today. At 8am, the ringgit opened easier at 3.9320/9370 against the greenback from Thursday's close of 3.9300/9330, although it traded firmer against other major currencies. Meanwhile, Mohd Afzanizam said the US Dollar Index rose 0.36 per cent to 98.874 points after April 2026 US retail sales data indicated consumer spending remained fairly resilient.

items.title: OpenAI explores legal options against Apple
items.summary: SAN FRANCISCO: Apple's two-year-old partnership with OpenAI has become strained, with the AI startup failing to see the expected benefits from its deal with the iPhone maker and preparing possible legal action, a person familiar with the matter said on Thursday. In 2024, Apple announced integration of its "Apple Intelligence" technology across its apps including Siri and bringing OpenAI's chatbot ChatGPT to its devices. Bloomberg News reported this month that Apple will allow users to select from third-party AI models and OpenAI could lose its unique role within Apple's software.

items.title: UK antitrust authority says launched investigation into Microsoft
items.summary: MOSCOW: The UK Competition and Markets Authority (CMA) said it had launched an investigation into US tech giant Microsoft, reported Sputnik/RIA Novosti. "The Competition and Markets Authority (CMA) has today launched a strategic market status (SMS) investigation into Microsoft's business software ecosystem," the authority said in a statement on Thursday. The CMA announced that the investigation will be completed within nine months, during which the authority will examine how Microsoft provides a range of software products used by UK organisations, including productivity software, operating systems for personal computers and servers, database management systems and security software.

items.title: 'Untouchable' Bukit Bintang karaoke club raided; hundreds of foreigners arrested
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: The Immigration Department raided a luxury karaoke entertainment centre inside a hotel along Jalan Changkat, Bukit Bintang, early this morning, arresting hundreds of foreigners. The Immigration Department said it had conducted intelligence gathering on the premises over the past two weeks. The outlet, which opened earlier this year, was allegedly a hub for foreigners committing various immigration offences.

items.title: MAHB optimistic on outlook, urgers greater aviation-tourism collaboration to boost passenger traffic
items.summary: Its managing director Datuk Mohd Izani Ghani said airport operators, airlines, regulators and tourism-related agencies coming together and pulling in the same direction will be crucial in sustaining industry resilience and ensuring the success of the Visit Malaysia 2026 premier national tourism campaign amid current global challenges. Mohd Izani said close cooperation among stakeholders has become increasingly important as the nation celebrates Visit Malaysia 2026, particularly in ensuring seamless travel experience, stable airline operations and stronger international connectivity. It works quite well," he said.

items.title: Environment Dept cites national security in restricting EIA report downloads
items.summary: PUTRAJAYA, May 15 — Full Environmental Impact Assessment (EIA) reports are not made publicly available for download to safeguard ownership and copyright protection, information security, and national security interests, the Department of Environment (DOE) said in a statement. It said EIA reports usually contain detailed technical information, including data on strategic locations, critical infrastructure, development plans, site coordinates and other sensitive material that require controlled access. The DOE added that access to the Executive Summary of EIA reports for public reference is available through designated platforms based on submitted applications.

items.title: Equities advance with tech boost, dollar rises after data
items.summary: NEW YORK/LONDON: Strong demand for technology stocks lifted equity indexes around the world on Thursday, while the dollar rose after economic data and investors awaited the outcome of a US-China summit. The S&P 500 rose 56.99 points, or 0.77 per cent, to 7,501.24 and the Nasdaq Composite rose 232.88 points, or 0.88 per cent, to 26,635.22, marking record closes for both. MSCI's gauge of stocks across the globe rose 6.25 points, or 0.56 per cent, to 1,115.56.

items.title: Foreign-born child adoption allowed if legal safeguards met, court rules
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: The High Court in Penang has ruled that foreign-born children without birth certificates may still be adopted, provided the legal requirements are met and the child's welfare is protected. Judge Dr Choong Yeow Choy held that procedural technicalities under the Adoption Act must not override the best interests of a child who has already been raised in a stable and loving home. On March 26, 2025, the couple filed an originating summons seeking an adoption order under the Adoption Act, along with consequential orders for a new birth certificate.

items.title: Grab engineer Mohammad Hasif Afiq becomes first Malaysian in Apple’s global developer honours programme
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, May 15 — Software engineer Mohammad Hasif Afiq has made history as the first Malaysian to be featured in Apple’s newly introduced Developer Recognition programme. Hasif described the recognition from the global tech giant as a surreal and emotional moment, after being notified via an official email from Apple’s Developer Relations team yesterday. Hasif said that over the years, he has developed several applications, including Amalan Mukmin, Vacay, Hidayah App, Lylyn App and Bantu App, with some gaining strong traction locally, especially the Bantu App during the COVID-19 pandemic.

items.title: Knife found beside dead technician in Johor Bahru apartment, police suspect murder
items.summary: JOHOR BAHRU, May 15 — A male technician was found dead, believed to have been murdered, in an apartment here Wednesday. Johor Bahru Selatan district police chief ACP Raub Selamat said his team received a report regarding the incident at around 11.12 pm. He said the initial investigation found that the victim was 33 years old.

items.title: DoE: Full EIA reports not available for download over security concerns
items.summary: PUTRAJAYA: Full Environmental Impact Assessment (EIA) reports are not made available for public download to safeguard copyright, information security and national security interests, says the Department of Environment (DoE). In a statement, it said that the EIA reports contained detailed technical data, including strategic site locations, critical infrastructure details, development plans, coordinates and other sensitive information requiring controlled access. The DoE added that executive summaries of EIA reports were available for public reference via designated platforms upon application.

items.title: ‘Mushroom-flavoured’ vape liquid dispute ends in fatal stabbing at Melaka playground, four suspects in custody
items.summary: JASIN, May 15 — A 30-year-old man died after being stabbed during a dispute over ‘mushroom-flavoured’ vape liquid at a playground in Merlimau last night, police said. Jasin police chief Supt Lee Robert said the victim suffered a stab wound to the right side of his back that pierced his lung, as well as bleeding to the head. “The victim died while receiving treatment at Melaka Hospital at 1.26 pm today.

items.title: LVMH sells Marc Jacobs to WHP Global, which will form partnership with G-III
items.summary: NEW YORK: French luxury giant LVMH will sell its Marc Jacobs brand to US brand firm WHP Global, the two companies said Thursday. Marc Jacobs, acquired by LVMH in 1997, will keep the namesake American designer as creative director once the transaction is finalised. According to the statement from LVMH and WHP Global, the agreement will see G-III operate Marc Jacobs' direct-to-consumer and wholesale businesses.

items.title: Promised profits in 30 minutes, woman loses nearly RM100,000 to Telegram investment scams
items.summary: KUALA TERENGGANU: A woman lost nearly RM100,000 in just two weeks to two non-existent investment schemes. Kuala Terengganu police chief Assistant Commissioner Azli Mohd Noor said the 38-year-old civil servant lodged a police report at the Kuala Terengganu police station about 2.50pm yesterday. Azli said she carried out 12 transactions to four different bank accounts amounting to RM48,965 in total.

items.title: Japan April wholesale inflation rate spikes on rising import costs
items.summary: TOKYO: Japan's wholesale prices in April rose a faster-than-expected 4.9 per cent from a year earlier, data showed on Friday, highlighting mounting inflationary pressures from the Iran war-induced spike in oil costs. It accelerated sharply from a revised 2.9 per cent gain in March. The yen-based import price index spiked 17.5 per cent in April from a year earlier after a revised 8.0 per cent gain in May, the data released by the BOJ showed.

items.title: Media barred from vape nicotine court ruling despite open proceedings
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: Journalists were barred today from covering the delivery of decisions in a judicial review challenging the government's move to remove liquid nicotine from the poisons list, despite the proceedings being conducted in open court. The decisions, scheduled at 2.45pm via video conferencing before Court of Appeal judge Datuk Aliza Sulaiman, sitting as a High Court judge, involve issues of constitutional and public interest. Under the principle of open justice, court proceedings are generally accessible to the public and media unless the court specifically orders otherwise.

items.title: US SEC settles civil lawsuit against Indian billionaire Adani subject to court approval
items.summary: WASHINGTON: The US Securities and Exchange Commission on Thursday settled a civil lawsuit against Indian billionaire Gautam Adani, subject to court approval, court records showed. The Justice Department is close to dropping related criminal fraud charges against Adani, who has promised to invest US$10 billion in the US economy, according to two sources familiar with the matter. © New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

items.title: German Mittelstand bets big on JS-SEZ as Johor emerges industrial gateway
items.summary: Germany's Ambassador to Malaysia Silke Riecken-Daerr said the JS-SEZ's biggest strength lies in its ability to combine Singapore's position as a global financial and logistics powerhouse with Johor's industrial land availability, lower operating costs, maritime access and multilingual workforce. She said the "twinning model" across the Causeway was becoming increasingly attractive to German companies, particularly the Mittelstand. She said about 90 German companies had already established operations in Johor and were encouraged by the state's business-friendly investment climate and long-term growth prospects.

items.title: Johor rolls out early aid to ease burden on hawkers, traders
items.summary: JOHOR BARU: The Johor government has rolled out a series of targeted measures, including capped Ramadan bazaar rentals, reduced local council charges and direct cash aid, to help hawkers and small traders cope with rising operating costs and economic pressures. Welcoming recent remarks by Housing and Local Government Minister Nga Kor Ming on assisting traders, Jafni said Johor had already implemented "aggressive and targeted" interventions. Since January, he said, the state had capped Ramadan bazaar lot rentals at RM500 in several major local councils, including Johor Baru City Council, Iskandar Puteri City Council, Pasir Gudang City Council and Kulai Municipal Council.

items.title: Scientists dig up Southeast Asia's largest dinosaur in Thailand
items.summary: WASHINGTON: Along a meandering river in a warm and arid region that is now Thailand roughly 113 million years ago, a plant-eating behemoth almost 90 feet (27 meters) long browsed on the treetops without much fear of predators due to its sheer size. This was Nagatitan chaiyaphumensis, the largest-known dinosaur from Southeast Asia. Researchers have unearthed skeletal remains of Nagatitan, a member of the dinosaur lineage called sauropods known for having a long neck, long tail, small head and four columnar legs.

items.title: Wall Street ends higher on tech rally; investors eye Beijing talks
items.summary: NEW YORK: US stocks advanced on Thursday, lifted by a rally in tech stocks as investors absorbed generally solid economic data and watched for developments from Beijing, where US President Trump was engaged in a high-stakes meeting with his Chinese counterpart Xi Jinping. All three major US stock indexes gained ground, with the S&P 500 and the Nasdaq setting their latest in a series of record closing highs. Nvidia's shares closed 4.4 per cent higher after the US cleared the sales of the company's H200 chips to Chinese firms.

items.title: Ship reported seized off UAE as Trump discusses Iran with Xi
items.summary: WASHINGTON: A ship was reported seized by Iranian personnel off the United Arab Emirates and headed for Iranian waters on Thursday while the White House said US President Donald Trump and Chinese President Xi Jinping had agreed on the need to keep the nearby Strait of Hormuz shipping lane open. China is close to Iran and is the main buyer of its oil. Iran has largely shut the strait to ships apart from its own since the US-Israeli war on Iran began on February 28, causing the biggest-ever disruption to global energy supplies.

items.title: Collaborating under pressure
items.summary: WORKING with new people on a time-lined project at work is both a challenge and an opportunity. When a group of unfamiliar individuals comes together, each person brings different experiences, assumptions, and working styles. Clearly defining the project's objectives, scope, and timeline helps create a shared understanding.

items.title: Property market slows in Q1, average house price at RM507,533
items.summary: According to the Valuation and Property Services Department (JPPH), transaction volume fell eight per cent year-on-year, while transaction value slipped marginally 0.6 per cent compared with the first quarter of 2025. Abdul Razak said the residential subsector remained the key driver of the property market. Completed residential units increased by more than 30 per cent to 12,905 units compared with 9,329 units in the same quarter last year.

items.title: Sultan Nazrin decrees Teachers' Day Friday sermons led by educators
items.summary: IPOH: The Sultan of Perak, Sultan Nazrin Muizzuddin Shah, has decreed that Friday sermons across the state will focus on teachers in conjunction with the upcoming Teachers' Day celebration. Mohd Annuar said several senior education figures would also deliver Friday sermons at selected mosques across the state. "Education Ministry director-general Datuk Dr Mohd Azam Ahmad will deliver the sermon at the Sultan Idris Shah II State Mosque, while the ministry's Islamic Education Division director Omar Salleh will speak at the Sultan Azlan Shah Mosque in Ipoh.

items.title: Sultan Nazrin decrees Teachers' Day Friday sermons led by educators
items.summary: IPOH: The Sultan of Perak, Sultan Nazrin Muizzuddin Shah, has decreed that Friday sermons across the state will focus on teachers in conjunction with the upcoming Teachers' Day celebration. Mohd Annuar said several senior education figures would also deliver Friday sermons at selected mosques across the state. "Education Ministry director-general Datuk Dr Mohd Azam Ahmad will deliver the sermon at the Sultan Idris Shah II State Mosque, while the ministry's Islamic Education Division director Omar Salleh will speak at the Sultan Azlan Shah Mosque in Ipoh.

items.title: Florida's Palm Beach airport to be renamed for Trump effective July 9
items.summary: FLORIDA: The Federal Aviation Administration said Palm Beach International Airport in Florida will be officially renamed after Donald Trump effective July 9, the latest in a series of buildings, institutions, government programs, warships and money to bear the US president's name. Transportation Secretary Sean Duffy said earlier the airport's three-letter code will be changed from PBI to DJT denoting Trump's initials. © New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

items.title: Fatah congress re-elects Mahmoud Abbas as movement leader
items.summary: RAMALLAH: Members of the Eighth General Conference of the Fatah Movement unanimously re-elected President Mahmoud Abbas on Thursday as the leader of the movement, Palestine News & Info Agency (WAFA) reported. Abbas, the elected President of the State of Palestine and Chairman of the Executive Committee of the Palestine Liberation Organisation (PLO), is also regarded as one of the founding members of Fatah alongside its early pioneers. Abbas added that renewing the movement through new leadership would strengthen the broader national movement and enhance its achievements.

items.title: Failure to launch: Norway confirms no missile deal for Malaysia
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: Malaysia's fallout with Norway over a cancelled missile deal deepened yesterday after Oslo confirmed it had revoked export licences tied to the Naval Strike Missile (NSM) system, citing tighter restrictions on some of its most sensitive defence technologies amid a shifting global security landscape. In a response to the New Straits Times, Norway's Foreign Ministry said the revocation was linked to stricter export control regulations that now limit certain advanced Norwegian-developed defence technologies to the country's allies and "closest partners". "The Ministry of Foreign Affairs can confirm that certain licences related to the export of specific defence technology to Malaysia have been revoked.

items.title: Musk accused of 'selective amnesia,' Altman of lying as OpenAI trial nears end
items.summary: "Mr. Musk may have the Midas touch in some areas, but not in AI," said William Savitt, a lawyer for OpenAI. Musk is suing OpenAI and Altman for breach of charitable trust and unjust enrichment. Musk is seeking about US$150 billion in damages from OpenAI and Microsoft, which would be paid to OpenAI's nonprofit.

items.title: Khaled: No sign of Norway rescinding export ban [WATCH]
items.summary: Defence Minister Datuk Seri Mohamed Khaled Nordin said there have so far not been any positive indication that the Norwegian government will go back on its decision to revoke the export permit for the surface-to-surface missiles to Malaysia. [[nid:1439872]] On Wednesday, Khaled had said he would meet his Norwegian counterpart, Tore O. Sandvik, in Singapore later this month when they attend the annual Shangri-La Dialogue. Khaled said the LCS programme would proceed as scheduled despite the NSM issue.

items.title: Kopitiam nostalgia goes mainstream as Malaysia’s cafe culture turns back the clock
items.summary: Inside, customers linger over kopi and kaya toast, some chatting in groups while others sit alone with laptops open — scenes once closely associated with international coffee chains. Across urban shopping malls, kopitiam -style chains such as Oriental Kopi, Nanyang Cafe and Hock Kee Heritage Kopitiam are repackaging an imagined version of old Malaya into a contemporary café experience built around familiarity and nostalgia. Genevieve Lim, 28, said modern kopitiam chains combine local food with the practical comforts younger consumers increasingly expect from contemporary cafés.

items.title: MACC confirms getting USB drive, documents from Rafizi for ARM deal probe
items.summary: PUTRAJAYA, May 14 — The Malaysian Anti-Corruption Commission (MACC) confirmed it has received several documents and video recordings from former Economy Minister Datuk Seri Rafizi Ramli to assist in investigations into alleged abuse of power and misappropriation involving government contracts worth nearly RM1.1 billion. MACC Senior Director of the Investigation Division said Rafizi arrived with five lawyers and handed over printed documents and video recordings in a USB flash drive, believed to be related to ARM Limited UK. Mohd Hafaz said the MACC also recorded additional statements from Rafizi, and the process ended at 1.30 pm.

items.title: Sungai Buloh police issue appeal over two missing 15-year-old girls
items.summary: Sungai Buloh district police chief Supt Mohd Hafiz Muhammad Nor said a report on the disappearance of Nur Amani Damia Roslan was received at 3.54 pm yesterday from her father. He said Nur Amani Damia is 150 centimetres (cm) tall, weighs 60 kilogrammes (kg) and was last seen wearing a dark-coloured shirt and pants. Mohd Hafiz said police are also actively searching for another teenager, Angel Ho Kha Suen, who has not returned home since Sunday.

items.title: Vigneswaran: MIC’s 80th anniversary celebration to feature community programmes nationwide
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, May 14 — Various programmes will be held nationwide from May 21 in conjunction with MIC’s 80th anniversary celebration to encourage greater participation from both party members and the public, said its president Tan Sri S A Vigneswaran. Vigneswaran said despite facing political and organisational challenges over the years, MIC had continued serving the community throughout its eight decades of existence. He said MIC has carried out numerous welfare and community initiatives including educational assistance, medical programmes, food aid distribution and social support efforts.

items.title: Malaysia opens public university access for tahfiz, UEC, private school graduates
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, May 14 — The Cabinet has approved a new, alternative entry pathway for students from religious schools, private schools, and Chinese independent secondary schools into the nation's public universities (IPTAs). First, students from these streams who possess a full Sijil Pelajaran Malaysia (SPM) certificate may apply for any study programme offered through the standard UPUOnline system. Meanwhile, for graduates of Chinese independent secondary schools, the available courses focus on Chinese language and cultural studies, such as a Bachelor of Arts in Chinese Language Studies and a Bachelor of Chinese Language and Linguistics.

items.title: Fahmi quashes talk of GE16 soon, says PM’s focus is on governance
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, May 14 — Communications Minister Fahmi Fadzil has dismissed speculation about an early 16th General Election (GE16) this year, saying Prime Minister Datuk Seri Anwar Ibrahim has given no such indication. Fahmi, who also serves as the unity government's spokesman, said the PM’s priority remains squarely on addressing the public's problems and implementing reforms, The Star reported. Looking ahead, Fahmi highlighted the significance of the unity government's national convention in Johor this weekend, stating it is expected to set the electoral tone for the coalition.

items.title: Pardon? Fahmi says Jho Low must face justice in Malaysia over 1MDB
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, May 14 — Communications Minister Datuk Fahmi Fadzil has echoed the view of 1Malaysia Development Berhad (1MDB) Task Force chairman Datuk Seri Johari Abdul Ghani that fugitive businessman Low Taek Jho, better known as Jho Low, should not be pardoned following reports that he is seeking clemency from the United States authorities. Fahmi, who is also the MADANI Government spokesperson, said Jho Low should return to Malaysia to face trial over the 1MDB financial scandal. According to the report, approval of the application would result in the US criminal charges against Jho Low being dropped.

items.title: Pressed by Guan Eng on Penang polls, Kon Yeow grabs Hokkien reprieve from PAS rep
items.summary: GEORGE TOWN, May 14 — The decision on whether Penang will hold its state poll concurrently with the next general election hinges on two key factors: the state constitution and the final say of the ruling party’s leadership, Chief Minister Chow Kon Yeow said today. In response to a question from Lim Guan Eng (PH - Air Putih) at the state legislative assembly, Chow explained that he could not make a unilateral decision. “We need to look at this from two angles: one will be based on the constitution, and another will be based on the political party’s stand,” Chow said.

items.title: Toddler critical after falling five floors at Johor Bahru PPR flat
items.summary: Johor Bahru North police chief Assistant Commissioner Radin Ramlan Radin Taha said authorities were alerted to the incident at 10:30 am. “The victim suffered serious injuries to his face, head, and neck from the fall,” Radin Ramlan said in a statement. “During the incident, the window was found to be slightly open and unlocked,” Radin Ramlan added.

items.title: Minister teases special announcements by PM at National Youth Day tomorrow
items.summary: REMBAU, May 14 — Prime Minister Datuk Seri Anwar Ibrahim is expected to make several special announcements for youths during the National Youth Day (HBN) 2026 celebration at X Park Sendayan tomorrow. Youth and Sports Minister Datuk Seri Dr Mohammed Taufiq Johari said the prime minister is scheduled to officiate the three-day programme themed ‘Empowering MADANI Youth’ at 9 am. Earlier, Mohammed Taufiq officiated the closing ceremony and prize presentation for the 38th Institute of Youth and Sports Skills Training (ILKBS) sports championship held at the National Youth Advanced Skills Institute Chembong (IKTBN Chembong) here.

items.title: MACC records once indicated insufficient evidence against Guan Eng in tunnel probe, court told
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, May 14 — Earlier Malaysian Anti-Corruption Commission (MACC) investigation records into the Penang undersea tunnel project had indicated there was insufficient evidence against former Penang chief minister Lim Guan Eng before further investigations were carried out in 2020, according to The Star. According to the report, MACC investigating officer Zulhilmi Ramli told the Sessions Court today that he had initially joined a task force in early 2020 to review earlier statements and documents linked to the project following a complaint lodged in 2018. Zulhilmi reportedly said that he only took over as investigating officer in June 2020, after several earlier officers had handled different investigation papers relating to the project.

items.title: No insurance, no provocation, no warning: The victim’s side of the Kepong bak kut teh assault (VIDEO)
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, May 14 — The victim of the now-viral assault at a Kepong bak kut the restaurant has spoken out for the first time, revealing that the brutal attack was completely unprovoked and has left him traumatised, in physical pain, and fearful for his family’s safety. He said he was quietly eating his meal when he heard a noise from a nearby table of men who were drinking. “I looked up and saw some guys drinking, then turned away to continue eating,” he explained.

items.title: Tabung Haji confirms five Malaysian deaths in Holy Land amid ongoing Haj season
items.summary: MAKKAH, May 14 — The number of Malaysian pilgrims who have died in the Holy Land during this year’s Haj season has risen to five following two additional deaths involving pilgrims from flight group KT12. In a statement today, Lembaga Tabung Haji (TH) said both pilgrims, who were staying at Al Kiswah Hotel, died on Monday and Wednesday respectively. The statement said a 72-year-old woman from Sitiawan, Perak who travelled to the Holy Land with her cousin to perform the Haj pilgrimage, died on Monday.

items.title: Driver in fatal Jalan Ampang crash to be charged with murder tomorrow
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, May 14 — The man suspected of causing a crash that killed two people along Jalan Ampang earlier this month will be charged tomorrow with two counts of murder. According to the New Straits Times (NST), Kuala Lumpur Traffic Enforcement and Investigation Department chief ACP Mohd Zamzuri Mohd Isa said the 24-year-old man is scheduled to be charged under Section 302 of the Penal Code at the Kuala Lumpur Court at 9am tomorrow. It was previously reported that the married couple’s motorcycle was struck from behind by a car driven by the suspect along Jalan Ampang.

items.title: Air rage lands passenger RM5,000 fine for assaulting traveller, spitting at crew on KL–Kuching flight
items.summary: KUCHING, May 14 — The Magistrates’ Court here today fined a man RM5,000 in default five months’ jail for punching a passenger, spitting on a flight crew member, and causing a disturbance on board an aircraft. Magistrate Nursyaheeqa Nazwa Radzali meted out the sentence on Mohd Izzan Mohd Hashim, 36, after he pleaded guilty to a charge under Regulation 102 of the Civil Aviation Regulations 2016. Police investigations found witnesses stating that Mohd Izzan acted aggressively while on board the aircraft by punching a passenger in the face, spitting on a flight crew member, and causing a disturbance.

items.title: Police warn of elephant danger after man found dead in Gua Musang plantation
items.summary: GUA MUSANG, May 14 — A 55-year-old man was found dead today, believed to have been attacked by a herd of wild elephants near a riverbank close to a rubber plantation along Jalan Ladang Serasa here. Gua Musang police chief Supt Sik Choon Foo said police received a report on the discovery of the victim’s body at 11.38am. He said a police team from the Paloh 2 police station was dispatched to the scene to conduct investigations.

items.title: KL Tower set to glow blue on May 18 for International Museum Day
items.summary: PUTRAJAYA, May 14 — The Kuala Lumpur Tower (Menara KL) will be illuminated in blue on May 18 in conjunction with International Museum Day 2026, which is observed worldwide. It added that this year’s International Museum Day is celebrated under the theme “Museums Uniting a Divided World”. The ministry said the blue lighting symbolises support for efforts to empower museums as institutions of knowledge, heritage and education.

items.title: Three men face assault charges over Kepong restaurant attack sparked by alleged side‑eye incident
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, May 14 — Three men, whose assault on a man at a restaurant in Kepong went viral on social media after they claimed he gave them the ‘side eye’, were charged in the Sessions Court today with causing hurt to the victim. They were jointly charged with intentionally causing grievous hurt to Andrew Teh Tiek Hua, 49, at a restaurant on Jalan Kepong, Metro Prima, at 2am on May 7. “Although this case has gone viral, it does not automatically mean the accused should be denied bail.

items.title: Court charges babysitter with murdering seven‑month‑old baby at Puchong nursery
items.summary: PETALING JAYA, May 14 — A babysitter was charged in the Magistrates’ Court today with murdering a seven-month-old baby boy at a nursery in Bandar Kinrara, Puchong, earlier this month. The woman is accused of causing the death of Muhammad Eiden Faheem Mohammed Nor Syafiq at a nursery in Kinrara Commercial Unit, Bandar Kinrara, Puchong, between 9.30am and 10.40am on May 5. Deputy public prosecutor Ainul Athirah Jamalullail appeared for the prosecution, while lawyer Datuk Suraj Singh represented the accused.

items.title: Court commutes death sentence to 40 years’ jail for ex‑student who murdered pregnant girlfriend, set body on fire
items.summary: A three-man bench, comprising Justices Datuk Azman Abdullah, Datuk Ahmad Kamal Md Shahid and Datuk Seri Mohd Radzi Harun, allowed Muhammad Fakrul Aiman Sajali’s appeal against his sentence, substituting the High Court’s death penalty with a custodial sentence and caning. He ordered the sentence to take effect from May 23, 2023, the date of Muhammad Fakrul’s arrest. Earlier, his lawyer Muhammad Nor Tamrin submitted that the death sentence imposed by the High Court was too harsh, stressing that the appellant was a young offender.

items.title: Malaysia weighs legal action after Norway blocks missile export for LCS programme
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, May 14 — The Defence Ministry is considering legal action after Norway moved to block the export of Naval Strike Missiles (NSM) for Malaysia’s littoral combat ship (LCS) programme. According to Malaysiakini , Defence Minister Datuk Seri Mohamed Khaled Nordin said the government had already paid nearly 95 per cent of the contract value, amounting to more than RM500 million. Khaled said the LCS programme would continue and the ships would still be completed, although they would not have surface-to-surface missiles until Malaysia secures a replacement system.

items.title: Namewee freed of drug charges in Kuala Lumpur court, AGC drops case after second bid
items.summary: PETALING JAYA, May 14 — Singer Wee Meng Chee, better known as Namewee, was today acquitted by the magistrates’ court of two drug charges. Last month, the AGC had rejected Wee’s initial representation to withdraw the charges under Section 9(1) of the Poisons Act 1952 and Section 39A(1) of the Dangerous Drugs Act 1952. In January, the 43-year-old pleaded not guilty to possessing 0.78g of sildenafil under the Poisons Act 1952 and an amended charge of possessing 1.57g of methamphetamine under the Dangerous Drugs Act 1952.

items.title: Malaysia’s growth forecast at 4.4pc in 2026, risks from global conflict and trade tensions
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, May 14 — The World Bank projects Malaysia’s economy to record solid growth of 4.4 per cent this year, with domestic demand continuing to anchor activity. He said that as a highly open economy, Malaysia remains exposed especially through trade and financial channels. At the same time, Mustafaoglu highlighted that Malaysia is facing an important challenge: while employment remains strong, productivity growth is modest.

items.title: Cleaner charged in Manjung Magistrate’s Court with murder of elderly man during house robbery
items.summary: MANJUNG, May 14 — A cleaner was charged in the Magistrate’s Court today with the murder of an elderly man during a robbery at the victim’s house last week. M. Manimaran, 27, nodded in understanding after the charge was read out before Magistrate Asma’ Nahdiyya Che Ab Rahim. The man was charged with intentionally causing the death of Tang Kai Kong, 85, at Jalan Melayu, Ayer Tawar, Manjung, between 3am and 4am on May 7.

items.title: Malaysia’s population rises to 34.4m in Q1 2026, DOSM says growth slows to 0.5pc as ageing trend accelerates
items.summary: “Meanwhile, the young age population (0-14 years) recorded a decrease from 21.8 per cent to 21.6 per cent. Sabah Bumiputera contributed 55.3 per cent of other Bumiputera, which comprised Kadazan/Dusun (31.7 per cent), Bajau (26.5 per cent), Murut (5.1 per cent) and other Sabah Bumiputera (36.7 per cent). Meanwhile, Sarawak Bumiputera, which made up 32.3 per cent, consisted of Iban (59.1 per cent), Bidayuh (17.1 per cent), Melanau (10.6 per cent) and other Sarawak Bumiputera (13.2 per cent).

items.title: Penang Opposition leader hospitalised after blood pressure spike during state assembly sitting
items.summary: GEORGE TOWN, May 14 — Penang Opposition leader Muhammad Fauzi Yusoff was taken to the hospital in the middle of the state legislative assembly sitting today after experiencing a spike in blood pressure. The Sungai Dua assemblyman was experiencing some discomfort during the lunch break of the state legislative assembly sitting today. He said Muhammad Fauzi’s blood pressure was found to be higher than normal, and he was sent to Penang Hospital for further checks and observation.
